questions.question: What are two words that come to your mind when you think of friendship?
How many words can be found in the grid related to friendship?
What is the first letter of each word in the grid marked with a bold letter?
What does 'p __ r __n __ r' mean in the context of friendship?
What is an informal word for friends?
Who offered Mario a fruit chaat as a bet?
What was the challenge given to Mario by his grandfather?
questions.correct_answer: to sit on an invisible chair

questions.question: Why did Mario's classmates laugh when he tried sitting on the chair?
questions.correct_answer: he missed and fell onto his backside

questions.question: What was 'The Chair' in the story?
questions.correct_answer: an invisible chair with magical powers

questions.question: Why did Mario's grandfather give him the task to sit on the chair?
questions.correct_answer: to find out who his real friends were

notes.title: Meanings of Words
notes.content: The story provides examples of words related to friendship, such as 'p __ r __n __ r' and 'b __ __ d __ __ s'.

notes.title: Types of Friends
notes.content: The story mentions different types of friends, including real friends, companions, and partners.

notes.title: Importance of Friendship
notes.content: Friendship is an important aspect of life, as seen in the story where Mario's grandfather helps him understand its value.

notes.title: Consequences of Bragging
notes.content: Mario's pride in having many friends leads to a challenge from his grandfather, teaching him a lesson about being humble.

notes.title: Magical Powers
notes.content: The invisible chair has magical powers that reveal real friends from fake ones.

notes.title: Companions vs. Real Friends
notes.content: The story highlights the difference between companions and real friends, as seen in Mario's case.

notes.title: Importance of Honesty
notes.content: Mario's grandfather teaches him to be honest about his relationships with others.

notes.title: Friendship is a Two-Way Street
notes.content: The story shows that friendship requires effort and commitment from both parties involved.

notes.title: Types of Relationships
notes.content: The story mentions different types of relationships, including friendships and family bonds.

notes.title: Real Friends vs. Fake Ones
notes.content: Mario's grandfather helps him understand the difference between real friends and fake ones.

facts.fact: The story mentions eight words related to friendship that can be found in a grid.
Mario has many friends at school, but he is unsure if they are all real friends.
Mario's grandfather gives him an invisible chair with magical powers to find out who his real friends are.
The story highlights the importance of being humble and not bragging about one's relationships.
Friendship is a vital aspect of life, as seen in the story where Mario learns a valuable lesson.
The invisible chair has magical powers that reveal real friends from fake ones.
Mario's grandfather helps him understand the difference between companions and real friends.
The story shows that friendship requires effort and commitment from both parties involved.
Mario learns to be honest about his relationships with others, thanks to his grandfather's guidance.
The story mentions different types of relationships, including friendships and family bonds.
